name: Iris Cook
bio: Iris Cook is pictured with the quote "Patience once more."

name: Gene Coplin
quote: I do not envy Aristotles wit, Nor do aspire to Caesar's bleeding fame.
bio: Gene Coplin was a member of the National Honor Society in 1931 and 1932.

name: Leo Corkotto
quote: My wealth is my health and perfect ease.
bio: Leo Corkotto participated in Football and Track in 1931, served as Reporter for the Booster Club in 1931, and was in Glee Club from 1929 to 1931.

name: Dansby Council
quote: Wilt thou show the wealth of thy wit in one instant.
bio: Dansby Council served as Class President in 1929, 1930, and 1932, and as Vice-president in 1931. He was President of the Spanish Club, a Catalinian, member of Student Council, and Assistant Business Manager of the Bruin.

name: Juanita Cox
quote: With gifts of wit and ornaments of nature.
bio: Juanita Cox was an Athenian from 1930 to 1932, a Glee Club member in 1931 and 1932, and participated in the Senior Play and Pep Squad.

name: Arthur Albert Crabtree
quote: My consciences clear, my chief defense.
bio: Arthur Albert Crabtree participated in Smith-Hughes Printing in 1931 and 1932, served as Make-up Editor of the Grizzly in 1931, and was on the Bruin Staff.

name: Gertrude Craddock
quote: Turn two steps into a manly stride, and speak of frays.
bio: Gertrude Craddock was a Columbian from 1930 to 1932, a Pep Squad member in 1931, and Cheer Leader in 1931.

name: Louise Craig
quote: Happiness courts thee in her best array.
bio: Louise Craig was an Athenian from 1929 through 1932.

name: Harris Crane
quote: What I lack, my mind supplies.
bio: Harris Crane participated in the Athletic Carnival, Chemistry Club, Band, Orchestra, and Contest Band during his school years.

name: William Crane
quote: When I am old there is grief enough for me.
bio: William Crane was a member of the Orchestra and Band from 1929 to 1932, played in the Contest Band, and was Drum-Major of the Band in 1932.
